symptoms.symptom_name: Sharp pleuritic retrosternal pain worse lying flat/on inspiration, relieved by sitting forward

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional pleuritic pain relieved by sitting forward is characteristic of pericarditis.
Positional, sharp, breathing-related pain is not the crushing, non-positional pain of ACS (though ECG/troponin confirm).
Trapezius-ridge radiation (phrenic referral) is fairly specific for pericardial irritation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent viral prodrome (sore throat, myalgias) about a week ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recent viral illness is the common trigger for viral/idiopathic pericarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing pain, no severe rest dyspnoea, no syncope, no leg swelling (no ACS/tamponade/myocarditis/PE)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of these complication features supports uncomplicated pericarditis suitable for community management after in-person confirmation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade fever with only partial relief from paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low-grade fever accompanies inflammatory pericarditis; paracetamol is typically insufficient (NSAIDs are needed).
A pericardial friction rub is the specific auscultatory sign of pericarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread ST-segment elevation and PR-segment depression on ECG (with normal/mildly raised troponin)

investigation_details.expected_result: Diffuse concave ST-segment elevation with PR-segment depression; a normal or only mildly raised troponin (a large rise would indicate myopericarditis) and a raised CRP
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Pericarditis is not confirmed and ACS/concurrent myocarditis are not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse ST elevation with PR depression is the classic ECG of pericarditis, distinct from the territorial ST elevation of STEMI.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Positional pleuritic pain relieved by sitting forward with trapezius radiation after a viral prodrome, with (sealed) a friction rub and no complication features, indicates uncomplicated acute pericarditis needing in-person ECG/troponin confirmation and NSAID/colchicine treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms diffuse ST elevation/PR depression and helps exclude a STEMI pattern.
Troponin screens for concurrent myocarditis (myopericarditis); CRP supports the inflammatory diagnosis and monitoring.
Assesses for a pericardial effusion and its haemodynamic significance if complication features arise.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Starting NSAIDs at home without any ECG/troponin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: NSAIDs are first-line for pericarditis, but ACS and concurrent myocarditis should be excluded with an in-person ECG/troponin before/with starting them.
